title: Relief-System Derating and HAZOP Safeguard Prioritization for Reactive Solvent Unit
expected_economic_value: $180k-$340k chemical process safety / relief systems consulting
normalized_title: Relief-System Derating and HAZOP Safeguard Prioritization for Reactive Solvent Unit
instruction: A chemical plant discovered flare-header backpressure and corrosion deposits that may derate relief capacity on a reactive solvent recovery vessel. Operations wants to restart in 36 hours, while process safety needs a defensible relief and HAZOP priority screen. Task packet: vessel V-302 volume=18.5 m3, MAWP=10.0 barg, relief_set_pressure=9.2 barg, allowable_accumulation=21%, relieving_temperature=156 C, molecular_weight=74, Z=0.91, existing_relief_area=18.4 cm2, discharge_coefficient=0.84, installed_orifice_label=P, normal_flare_backpressure=1.1 barg, measured_backpressure_after_fouling=2.6 barg, bellows_limit_backpressure=3.0 barg, corrosion deposit reduces effective_area by estimated 18% with uncertainty +/-6%, required_vapor_relief_area_scenarios: external_fire=12.6 cm2, blocked_outlet=15.9 cm2, cooling_water_failure=17.8 cm2, reactive_runaway_base=16.4 cm2, reactive_runaway_with_contamination=22.7 cm2. Reaction calorimetry excerpt: onset=138 C, adiabatic_temp_rise=86 C, time_to_max_rate at 150C=42 min, contamination iron_ppm 0 baseline, 35 ppm worst-case lowers onset by 11 C. HAZOP nodes and safeguards: N1 high_temperature cause steam_valve_fail_open consequence runaway safeguards TIC-302, high-high trip SIF-302 SIL1, operator response 12 min; N2 blocked_outlet cause downstream valve closed consequence overpressure safeguards PSV-302, carseal_open check weekly; N3 contamination cause wrong catalyst drum consequence lower_onset safeguards barcode_scan, lab_release; N4 flare_restriction cause fouled knock-out pot consequence PSV_capacity_loss safeguards monthly_dp_round, no alarm. Risk matrix: severity 5 fatality/site damage, 4 major injury, 3 recordable; likelihood A yearly=5, B 1/3yr=4, C 1/10yr=3, D 1/30yr=2, E remote=1; risk_score=severity*likelihood. Rules and constraints: effective_area=existing_area*(1-area_loss)*(1-0.015*(measured_backpressure-normal_backpressure)*10) but not below zero; relief scenario passes if effective_area>=required_area; restart prohibited if any credible scenario severity>=4 fails relief capacity or if contamination scenario has no independent safeguard; HAZOP priority is risk_score plus 8 if safeguard is administrative only, plus 10 if relief scenario fails, minus 5 for independent SIF; recommend safeguards with cost/cycle: flare_dp_alarm cost=$68k install=14d reduces N4 likelihood by 2, PSV_clean_replace cost=$42k install=2d restores area_loss to 0, catalyst_barcode_interlock cost=$55k install=10d reduces N3 likelihood by 2, temporary_batch_temp_limit_145C cost=$6k immediate reduces runaway required_area by 3.8 cm2 but reduces throughput 22%. Required work: deliver relief_derating_hazop_priority.xlsx + restart_safety_memo.md with effective-area calculation, scenario pass/fail, HAZOP ranked actions, restart decision, immediate temporary controls, and permanent safeguard roadmap. This task is self-contained: use the task packet as the factual record, do not add source references or external datasets, and preserve a high-fidelity audit trail for every calculation, classification, and recommendation. State assumptions and unresolved evidence gaps explicitly. When executed locally, place final deliverables under the artifacts/ directory.
allowed_tools: Local text editor or Markdown editor
Local spreadsheet, CSV, JSON, YAML, or scripting tools for deterministic calculations
Local plotting or diagramming tools when useful
disallowed_tools: External fact lookup or live data pulls
Live production systems, cloud accounts, trading systems, clinical systems, or industrial controls
Secret discovery, credential testing, or any action outside the prepared task workspace
input_requirements: Use the task packet as the sole factual input.
Cite row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, and option IDs from the packet wherever they control a conclusion.
Mark missing, contradictory, or under-supported values as needs_review instead of inventing them.
inputs: All factual inputs are embedded in the task packet.
Use row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, option IDs, and deliverable names exactly as provided.
output_requirements: Produce the required deliverable files with the specified filenames when filenames are provided.
Include intermediate calculations or executable logic sufficient for another reviewer to reproduce the result.
Include validation checks, sensitivity notes around controlling thresholds, and a final decision or recommendation table.
constraints: Use the task packet as the factual record.
Do not browse, call live services, import external datasets, or invent missing values.
Treat regulated or safety-critical outputs as review packets, not authorization to act.
quality_bar: Treat the task as realistic professional work with domain-appropriate rigor.
Preserve the packet's domain detail, identifiers, thresholds, and edge cases in the final deliverables.
Make every recommendation traceable to explicit task-packet evidence or mark it as needs_review.
validation_expectations: Check required fields, row counts, totals, thresholds, and classifications before finalizing.
Show enough formulas, script logic, or tables for an independent reviewer to reproduce the result.
Flag any contradiction, missing input, or threshold-sensitive conclusion.
edge_cases: If packet evidence is incomplete, do not infer missing values; label the item needs_review.
If multiple options satisfy hard constraints, rank them using the packet's stated priorities and explain ties.
If a regulated or safety-critical conclusion appears, frame the output as a review packet rather than authorization to act.
privacy_safety_expectations: Do not use live systems, private accounts, credentials, or external datasets.
Do not include raw secrets or operational instructions that would execute real-world changes.
Keep synthetic identifiers from the packet only when needed for verification.
